substrates.substrate_A: Various substrates including maleimides, dieneones, pyrroles, propellane
substrates.substrate_B: Various co-reactants depending on specific reaction

chemistry.product: Various cyclic products from [2+2] cycloadditions, [5+2] cycloadditions, and rearrangements
chemistry.bond_formed: Various C-C bonds through cycloaddition and rearrangement processes

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: Various excited states and photochemical intermediates

chemistry.scope_summary: Comprehensive study comparing batch vs flow photochemistry across 12 different reactions including [2+2] cycloadditions, [5+2] cycloadditions, Paternò-Büchi reactions, and di-π-methane rearrangements. UV wavelengths of 254 nm, 312 nm, and 365 nm were used with various lamp powers.

optimization_table.key_findings: yields were essentially identical in all comparative cases, three-layer reactors were 20% more productive than batch, one-layer reactors were 20% lower productivity than batch

figures.caption: Figure 1.  Batch and flow reactors used in this study. (a) Comparison of the footprints of a 400 W medium-pressure Hg lamp batch reactor and power supply (left) with equivalent three-layer FEP flow reactor and peristaltic pump (right). figures.light_source_position: external UV lamps positioned around reactors
figures.key_information: Two different photoreactor setups shown: (a) dual reactor system with 400W lamp power supplies and green UV illumination, (b) three-reactor system with blue UV illumination and electrode assemblies

figures.flow_path: Two inlet streams merge at T-mixer, flow through reactor coil, pass through BPR to collection vessel
figures.key_information: Simple continuous flow reactor setup with T-mixer for reagent mixing, coiled reactor section, and back pressure regulation
figures.caption: Figure 4 shows that due to the offset layering of the FEP tubing, the effective path length is likely to be significantly less. Combining this with the expected absorption of FEP tubing, one might expect batch to be superior to even the three-layer FEP reactor. Clearly, this is not the case. At present, the superior performance of the FEP reactor is not fully under- stood and may reflect more comp | Figure 4.  Light-transmission scenarios through one-layer and three-layer FEP reactors.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Lower productivity compared to multi-layer FEP reactors
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Three-layer FEP reactors showed ~20% higher productivity than batch with essentially identical yields
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor configuration from immersion well quartz reactors to multi-layer FEP tubing reactors
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Light source power (400W), reaction chemistry, and overall yields
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous processing with smaller reaction volumes

figure_descriptions: Comparison of batch and flow reactor footprints
Multiple reactor configurations with UV lamp setups
Flow reactor scheme with T-mixer and BPR

conflicts: Pass 3 indicated back_pressure_bar=null but Figure shows BPR present - resolved by setting BPR_present=true based on figure evidence
notes: Comprehensive photochemistry comparison study with good documentation of reactor configurations. Some quantitative parameters missing (temperatures, specific yields, flow rates) but overall experimental design and key findings are well captured. BPR presence inferred from reactor scheme figure despite not being explicitly mentioned in text.
